findings.title: Homepage title is just the brand
findings.recommendation: Fine for a known brand; consider "Brand — what you do" (~50–60 chars) to also rank for the value proposition.

findings.title: Meta description is short
findings.recommendation: Aim for 140–160 characters to use the full SERP snippet.

findings.title: Heading levels skip a level
findings.detail: An H1 is followed by an H3.
findings.recommendation: Keep heading nesting sequential (no H2→H4 jumps).

findings.title: Repetitive vocabulary
findings.recommendation: Vary wording and add substance; heavy repetition signals thin or spun content.

findings.title: Dominant topics detected
findings.detail: work (30), zine (27), learning (26), git (24), notes (22), why (22)  ·  phrases: machine learning, open source, profiler week

findings.title: Body may not reinforce the title topic
findings.detail: None of the title/H1 keywords appear among the page's most frequent body terms.
findings.recommendation: Make sure the main content actually develops the topic promised by the title and H1 (helps ranking and AI topical grounding).

findings.title: Incomplete Open Graph tags
findings.recommendation: Add og:title, og:description and og:image for rich social and chat-app link previews.

findings.title: No Twitter/X card type
findings.recommendation: Add twitter:card (e.g. summary_large_image) for X previews.

findings.title: No structured data (JSON-LD)
findings.detail: The page has no application/ld+json markup.
findings.recommendation: Add JSON-LD for the page's entity (Article, Product, Organization…) to unlock rich results and clarify meaning for AI search. (Least critical on listing/index pages.)

findings.title: Few question-based headings
findings.detail: 1 of 43 subheadings are phrased as questions.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite key H2/H3s as the questions users actually ask — AI engines match answers to question-shaped headings.

findings.title: No direct answer up top
findings.detail: The opening doesn't state a concise answer/definition.
findings.recommendation: Lead with a 1–2 sentence direct answer before the details; AI overviews lift the top summary.

findings.title: No llms.txt
findings.recommendation: Optionally publish /llms.txt to guide AI crawlers to your key content. Evidence for ranking impact is still thin — treat as low-cost, low-certainty.

findings.title: AI crawlers are blocked in robots.txt
findings.detail: Disallowed from the site root: GPTBot (OpenAI (ChatGPT training/browse)), Google-Extended (Google (Gemini / AI Overviews training)).
findings.recommendation: If you want visibility in AI answers, allow these agents. If the block is intentional (content protection), ignore this.
findings.evidence: GPTBot (OpenAI (ChatGPT training/browse)), Google-Extended (Google (Gemini / AI Overviews training))
